title: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository
description: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository on GitHub

items.content_html:
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Apache Kylin.

This issue affects Apache Kylin: from 4.0.0 through 5.0.2. You are fine as long as the Kylin's system and project admin access is well protected.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 5.0.3, which fixes the issue.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-61735
  • https://lists.apache.org/thread/yscobmx869zvprsykb94r24jtmb58ckh
  • https://github.com/apache/kylin/pull/2332
  • https://github.com/apache/kylin/commit/22eb8fd5dfdeffa3fc57bae6d5c82a019eece662
  • https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KYLIN-6082
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f6m8-qm7j-fh65

items.content_html:
Uncontrolled Resource Consumption in Elasticsearch while evaluating specifically crafted search templates with Mustache functions can lead to Denial of Service by causing the Elasticsearch node to crash.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-52979
  • https://discuss.elastic.co/t/elasticsearch-7-17-25-and-8-16-0-security-update-esa-2024-40/377709
  • https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/114002
  • https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/commit/cbde7f456d7ccd98556302fccf3238bb4557fc91
  • https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/commit/f9b6b57d1d0f76e2d14291c04fb50abeb642cfbf
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mm3m-5497-xggg
items.title: [org.elasticsearch:elasticsearch] Elasticsearch Uncontrolled Resource Consumption Vulnerability

items.content_html:

items.title: [org.elasticsearch:elasticsearch] Elasticsearch Uncontrolled Resource Consumption Vulnerability

items.content_html:
In Hazelcast before 5.3.0, configuration routines don't mask passwords in the member configuration properly. This allows Hazelcast Management Center users to view some of the secrets.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-33264
  • https://github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast/pull/24266
  • https://github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast/pull/24266/commits/80a502d53cc48bf895711ab55f95e3a51e344ac1
  • https://github.com/hazelcast/hazelcast/commit/74eed86c2b2b727148c442e98a01d0ca6941a49e
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5gj6-62g7-vmgf
items.title: [com.hazelcast:hazelcast] Hazelcast vulnerable to unmasked password exposure

items.content_html:

items.content_html:
Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel vulnerability in Apache Kylin.

This issue affects Apache Kylin: from 4.0.0 through 5.0.2.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 5.0.3, which fixes the issue.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-61733
  • https://lists.apache.org/thread/8wmcffly6gp50nmfw8j4w3hlmv843yo0
  • https://github.com/apache/kylin/pull/2336
  • https://github.com/apache/kylin/commit/8b2cb8c71bd9885d70dad4f1a9822e38d9949b8c
  • https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KYLIN-6081
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mr9j-4j48-xcm2

items.content_html:
Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties vulnerability in Apache Kylin. You are fine as long as the Kylin's system and project admin access is well protected.

This issue affects Apache Kylin: from 4.0.0 through 5.0.2.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 5.0.3, which fixes the issue.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-61734
  • https://lists.apache.org/thread/z705g7sn3g0bkchlqbo1hz1tyqorn4d2
  • https://github.com/apache/kylin/pull/2332
  • https://github.com/apache/kylin/commit/22eb8fd5dfdeffa3fc57bae6d5c82a019eece662
  • https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KYLIN-6082
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p86w-w5rh-m3hx
